title: persona update builds dynamic SQL SET clause from fixed set of trusted strings
description:
LOCATION: src/persona.rs:122-132

FINDING: `persona::update` builds a SET clause from a Vec of string literals like "display_name = ?" and "avatar_path = ?" that are hardcoded in the function. These are not user-controlled, so there is no SQL injection. However, the function uses `format!()` to construct the query string and `sqlx::query(&sql)` (the dynamic string form, not the compile-time macro). This pattern bypasses sqlx compile-time query checking. If a future contributor adds a user-controlled field name to the `fields` vec or passes a user-controlled string as the "clause" part, it would become SQL injection with no compile-time warning.

IMPACT: No immediate injection risk because the field clauses are hardcoded. The risk is maintainer-burden: the dynamic query construction pattern will be copied for future fields, and a contributor adding a user-supplied field name (e.g., from a JSON input) would introduce SQL injection without any compiler or linter warning.
close_reason: Not a bug: field names are hardcoded string literals from a fixed match, not user input. SQL injection is impossible. Added ponytail comment.
